summary: Kenneth demonstrates a weekly covered call strategy on HIMS, selling $1 OTM calls for ~$25 premium with the goal of $180-200/week. In this example, he made $300 when shares were assigned at $48 strike, combining $25 premium + $95 stock gain, and explains why rolling calls (buying back early) often doesn't make sense versus accepting assignment and restarting the cycle.
takeaways: Sell covered calls $1 out-of-the-money (OTM) on high-volatility stocks like HIMS to target $25 premium per weekly contract
If assigned, profit = premium collected + difference between purchase price and strike price; if not assigned, keep both premium and shares plus stock appreciation
Rolling/buying back contracts to prevent assignment costs capital (e.g., $400) and often destroys P&L; only roll if you have substantial unrealized gains or tax concerns worth the cost
Weekly covered calls work best on stocks with consistent volatility; as volatility decays, premiums shrink and you must find replacement stocks
Entry price matters for total return: buying at $47 and selling calls at $48 strike yields ~$95 profit on assignment plus $25 premium = $120 per round
Target $1 strike distance (OTM) balances high assignment probability with acceptable premium collection for consistent weekly income
Track weekly outcomes: update Monday on new purchase/sale, then Friday/Saturday on assignment or roll decision to maintain accountability.

strategy_candidates.change: Formalize HIMS weekly cycle into systematic desk rule: allocate $5K tranche to weekly $1 OTM covered calls targeting $25/week = $100/month incremental to $1,250/mo target, track assignment vs. roll discipline.
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth demonstrates repeatable $25/week premium on HIMS alone, making $300 in one week; extending to 4-5 different high-IV stocks at similar premiums would add $100-125/month reliable income to Premium Desk without complexity.

title: June 27, 2025 PT 4.5: Can I make $180 PER WEEK From HIMS Covered Calls?
